title: CostLibrary
description: A versioned cost library that the estimator-scheduler agent consults for unit rates. v0.1 ships ~150-200 hyperscale-DC rows seeded by an LLM (rate_source='llm_estimate', confidence 0.4-0.7). Charles can extend / override later via CSV upload. The library is NOT a PM artifact — it has its own top-level shape (no allOf base ref) so it can be loaded by the runtime as a flat data file.

version.description: Semantic version of the library (e.g. 'v0.1.0'). Bumped when rates change materially.

currency.description: v0 is USD-only. Multi-currency comes later.

base_year.description: Reference year for the unit rates. Estimator escalates from here.

geographic_basis.description: Default geography assumed (e.g. 'Central Ohio, USA'). Per-row overrides exist via geographic_multiplier_for.

notes.description: Free-form notes on scope, sources, methodology.

csi_section.description: MasterFormat 2020 section in the form 'NN NN NN' (e.g. '26 13 13'). Use the most specific section that fits.

unit.description: Unit of measure. Estimator uses these verbatim in the estimate rows.

unit_rate_usd.description: Cost per unit in USD at base_year.

rate_source.description: Provenance of the rate. The estimator must propagate this into estimate rows so reviewers can see what's a hard quote vs. an LLM-generated benchmark.

rate_year.description: Year the rate is dollar-denominated in. Estimator escalates to the project midpoint.

geographic_multiplier_for.description: Geography the rate is anchored to. Estimator may need to apply a city cost index multiplier for projects elsewhere.

confidence.description: Estimator's confidence in the rate. LLM-generated benchmarks should sit in 0.4-0.7.

notes.description: Inclusions / exclusions, vendor caveats, quantity-bracket assumptions.
